text: The cooling fan shall activate when the processor temperature exceeds 70°C
The system shall maintain the internal ambient temperature below 85°C during normal operation
Thermal management subsystem shall dissipate a minimum of 50W of heat continuously
The heat sink shall be constructed from anodized aluminum with a minimum surface area of 200 cm²
The system shall include a thermal cutoff switch that triggers at 95°C to prevent overheating
Temperature sensors shall be accurate to within ±1°C across the full operating range
The system shall log temperature readings every 5 seconds during operation
Fan speed shall be variable and proportional to the measured temperature above the activation threshold
The unit shall withstand a peak operating temperature of 90°C for up to 30 minutes
Thermal interface material shall be applied between the processor and heat sink with a coverage of at least 95%
The structure shall survive a shock load of 15g for 11 milliseconds in all three axes
The enclosure shall withstand a vibration level of 5 grms from 20 Hz to 2000 Hz
All fasteners shall be stainless steel Grade A4 with a minimum torque rating of 3 Nm
The chassis shall be manufactured from 6061-T6 aluminum alloy
The unit mass shall not exceed 2.5 kg including all connectors and mounting hardware
The PCB shall be secured using a minimum of 6 mounting points to prevent flexure under vibration
The enclosure shall maintain IP67 ingress protection rating under all operational conditions
The mechanical design shall allow for tool-free access to the battery compartment
All external edges shall have a minimum chamfer radius of 2 mm to prevent operator injury
The mounting bracket shall support a static load of 50 kg without permanent deformation
The software shall be developed to DO-178C Level B
All safety-critical software functions shall have 100% modified condition/decision coverage
The software shall perform a built-in test routine at startup and report any faults within 2 seconds
Software shall be partitioned so that a failure in the application layer cannot corrupt the safety layer
The real-time operating system shall schedule tasks with a maximum jitter of 100 microseconds
All software parameters shall be configurable via a protected configuration file without recompilation
The firmware shall support field updates via USB without requiring specialist tools
Software shall detect and log all hardware faults with a unique fault code and timestamp
The software watchdog timer shall trigger a system reset if the main loop does not complete within 500 ms
All inter-process communication shall use CRC-32 checksums to detect data corruption
The system shall comply with IEC 61508 SIL-2 for all safety-related functions
A failure mode and effects analysis shall be performed for every functional block
The system shall achieve a minimum MTBF of 50
All safety-critical inputs shall be validated through a two-out-of-three voting logic
The system shall fail safe to a known safe state upon detection of any unrecoverable fault
Safety functions shall have a diagnostic coverage of at least 90% as defined in IEC 61508
The system shall maintain its safety integrity level during single-point hardware failures
A proof test shall be required every 12 months to validate proper operation of all safety functions
The system shall prevent simultaneous actuation of conflicting outputs
Safety-critical signals shall be hardwired and shall not rely on software for their primary function
The system shall operate from a 28 VDC power supply with a tolerance of ±4 V
Power consumption during normal operation shall not exceed 35 W
The system shall include transient voltage suppression capable of handling 100V spikes lasting up to 50 ms
The unit shall draw no more than 200 mA inrush current during power-on
A reverse polarity protection circuit shall be included on the main power input
The power supply shall provide galvanic isolation between input and output of at least 500 VDC
Battery backup shall maintain operation for a minimum of 4 hours following primary power loss
Power supply efficiency shall exceed 85% at full load
The system shall generate a low power warning when the supply voltage drops below 24 VDC
All power rails shall be fused to prevent cascading failures
The system shall communicate via CAN bus at a data rate of 1 Mbit/s
All CAN messages shall include a message authentication code to detect tampering
The system shall support RS-422 serial communication at baud rates up to 115200
The Ethernet interface shall support 100BASE-TX and 1000BASE-T standards
All network communications shall use TLS 1.3 or later for encryption
The system shall support SNMP v3 for remote monitoring and management
Communication latency on the CAN bus shall not exceed 10 ms end-to-end
The system shall buffer up to 1000 messages in the event of a communication link failure
The communication interface shall support hot-plug insertion and removal without system reset
All external communication ports shall be protected against ESD up to ±15 kV
The system shall comply with MIL-STD-461G for electromagnetic emissions
The unit shall be immune to conducted susceptibility per MIL-STD-461G CS101
The enclosure shall provide a minimum of 40 dB shielding effectiveness from 100 MHz to 10 GHz
All cables shall be shielded with 95% optical coverage braid
The system shall meet DO-160G Section 20 for conducted emission limits
The PCB layout shall follow IPC-2141A guidelines for controlled impedance traces
Ferrite beads shall be placed on all power lines entering the PCB to attenuate high-frequency noise
The product shall pass radiated susceptibility testing at 200 V/m from 1 MHz to 18 GHz
The system shall comply with DO-160G Category B for electrostatic discharge
Ground planes shall be uninterrupted beneath all analog signal traces
The system shall operate continuously across an ambient temperature range of -40°C to +70°C
The unit shall survive storage at temperatures from -55°C to +85°C without performance degradation
The system shall operate at altitudes from sea level to 15
The unit shall withstand 95% relative humidity at 40°C for 96 hours without corrosion
The enclosure shall be rated to withstand salt fog exposure per ASTM B117
The system shall survive thermal cycling between -40°C and +70°C for a minimum of 100 cycles
All labels and markings shall remain legible after 1000 hours of UV exposure
The unit shall operate correctly after being immersed in water to a depth of 1 m for 30 minutes
The system shall operate in the presence of sand and dust at concentrations up to 10 mg/m³
Conformal coating shall be applied to all PCBs to protect against condensation and humidity
The user interface shall be compliant with WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards
The operator shall be able to acknowledge a fault alarm within 3 button presses or screen interactions
All status indicators shall be visible in direct sunlight with a minimum luminance of 300 cd/m²
The system shall display error messages in plain English without requiring interpretation of fault codes
The HMI shall require a 3-second confirmation hold to execute any irreversible action
The graphical display shall have a resolution of at least 800x480 pixels
The touchscreen shall detect input through standard nitrile work gloves
The system shall support multiple operator profiles with role-based access control
All alarm conditions shall be accompanied by both a visual and audible indicator
The HMI shall retain operator settings across power cycles without requiring re-entry
The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all configuration changes
Firmware images shall be cryptographically signed using RSA-4096 before deployment
The system shall enforce a minimum password length of 12 characters with complexity requirements
All failed authentication attempts shall be logged and reported after 5 consecutive failures
Physical access to internal connectors shall require breaking a tamper seal
The system shall support certificate-based mutual authentication for all network connections
Debug ports shall be permanently disabled in production firmware builds
All sensitive data shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256
The system shall support secure boot to prevent execution of unauthorized firmware
A secure element shall store all cryptographic keys to prevent extraction
The system shall be designed for a service life of 20 years with scheduled maintenance
Mean time to repair shall not exceed 4 hours using standard workshop tooling
All wear items shall be replaceable without requiring specialist calibration equipment
The system shall log all maintenance actions with operator identification and timestamp
Diagnostic software shall identify faulty line replaceable units to board level
The system shall provide prognostic health monitoring with 30-day advance warning of predicted failures
Spare parts availability shall be guaranteed for a minimum of 10 years post end-of-life
The system shall pass accelerated life testing equivalent to 20 years of operation
All critical components shall have a minimum component derating factor of 0.7
The system shall achieve zero failures in the first 90 days of operation (infant mortality period)
The system shall acquire a GPS fix within 60 seconds from cold start under open sky conditions
Position accuracy shall be within 2.5 m CEP 95% under nominal operating conditions
The navigation system shall continue to provide position estimates for 30 seconds following GPS signal loss
The system shall reject spoofed GPS signals with a false acceptance rate of less than 0.1%
Velocity measurement accuracy shall be within 0.1 m/s RMS
The inertial navigation unit shall have a gyroscope bias stability of less than 1 degree per hour
The system shall provide attitude estimates accurate to 0.1 degrees RMS in pitch and roll
Navigation data shall be output at a minimum rate of 10 Hz
The system shall detect and flag multipath interference using carrier-to-noise ratio thresholds
The navigation system shall comply with RTCA DO-229E for SBAS augmentation
